growth_records.strain: Species B (co-culture partner; also isolable in pure culture)

up.method: Species B does not oxidise MnCO3 alone; combined co-culture rate reported under Species A record

yields.cell_yield: 1.0e11 Species B cells per mol Mn(II) oxidized by the co-culture

growth_records.cite: Yu et al. Bacterial chemolithoautotrophy via manganese oxidation. Nature (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41586-020-2468-5. [PMC7802741]
growth_records.snippet: Concurrent with Mn(II) oxidation, Species A exhibited sustained exponential growth, roughly matched by the less numerous and perhaps commensal Species B (Td = 6.1 and 7.7 day, respectively).
growth_records.notes: Species B (Ramlibacter lithotrophicus) grows in the Mn(II)-oxidizing co-culture. Reported Td=7.7 day; mu=ln2/Td=0.693/(7.7*24 h)=0.693/184.8=0.00375 /h. Cell yield 1.0e11 cells per mol Mn(II) oxidized. Species B cannot oxidize MnCO3 alone; the combined Mn(II) uptake rate (3.4-9.0e-15 mol/cell/h) is a co-culture value logged under the Species A record. Species B is facultative and separately grows heterotrophically (succinate/acetate/tryptone) or autotrophically on H2+O2+CO2, but no quantitative rates given for those conditions.

phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 170921 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/170921
